topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt slick under my fingertips. It was heavy, dense, and the weight of the bills inside was almost suffocating. I stared at the stack of hundred-dollar notes, the sheer volume of untraceable wealth radiating off the paper. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, making my palms sweat. I quickly slid the wallet into my coat pocket, the fabric feeling suddenly unclean. I wanted to scrub my hands raw, to physically wipe the residue of that money off my skin. The sheer audacity of it—the careless disregard for the people who might need that—made my jaw clench so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I spent the rest of the afternoon avoiding eye contact, focusing instead on the grit under my fingernails, anything to distract from the heavy, metallic taste in my mouth.

[story 2]

When I pulled the wallet from under the bench, the crispness of the currency hit me like a physical blow. It was an obscene amount, a hoard of green rectangles that spoke of careless excess. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I took in the sheer magnitude of the cash. I felt a sudden, intense urge to recoil, to physically distance myself from the object. My gaze flickered over the numbers, and a wave of profound unease washed over me, leaving a clammy film on my skin. I quickly tucked it away, my movements jerky, almost panicked. "This is wrong," I muttered to myself, the words sounding thin and brittle in the quiet air. I kept rubbing my forearm repeatedly, a nervous habit born from the unsettling nature of what I was holding.

[story 3]

I found the wallet tucked beneath a loose paving stone. The contents were staggering; a veritable mountain of paper currency. As I counted the bundles, a profound sense of repulsion settled over me, heavy and unwelcome. My eyes darted away from the money, tracing patterns on the dusty ground instead. I felt a distinct tightening in my chest, a visceral rejection of the scene. I didn't want to touch it; the very proximity felt contaminated. I stood there for a long moment, just breathing in short, sharp gasps, my whole body rigid with an internal recoil. When I finally stuffed the wallet into my bag, my hands trembled visibly, and I felt a deep, churning nausea that had nothing to do with hunger.
